chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly states in Section VII (Implementation) that the authors implemented their ADKG protocol using Golang and provides links to external libraries used: Dedis Kyber (https://github.com/dedes/kyber), WaterBear BFT library (https://github.com/fififish/waterbear). However, it does not provide a direct link to a public repository containing the authors' own implementation code or datasets.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation was conducted on Amazon EC2 using up to 49 virtual machines (VMs) distributed across four regions. This constitutes a large-scale cloud deployment/emulation rather than a small toy example, though it relies on simulated network environments (virtual machines) rather than physical hardware testbeds.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper contains a dedicated Section III titled 'SYSTEM MODEL AND SYSTEM OVERVIEW' which formally defines the ADKG model. It explicitly states assumptions regarding asynchronous environments, the bound on faulty servers (t <= floor((n-1)/3)), and the lack of PKI or ROM dependencies.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous analysis in Section VII (Evaluation). It quantifies the latency improvement (2x-4.6x faster than DYX+), breaks down latency components (Figure 4b), and discusses communication complexity (O(lambda*n^3)) and message complexity, providing data-driven trade-off analysis.

major_weakness: The paper lacks a direct link to the authors' own implementation code or datasets, relying only on links to third-party libraries used in the evaluation.
